onboarding.question:
1. Define what UI state or message the user sees at the moment their logged-out session expires — whether a modal appears, an inline message, a redirect, or the chat simply becomes unresponsive.
2. Clarify whether the "Not signed in" indicator should reappear if a user's authentication state is lost mid-session (e.g., token expires server-side) or if re-authentication is required, and what the user experience is in that recovery moment.
3. Decide whether the in-conversation nudge (AC#10) should appear once per session or once per user lifetime — the spec says "exactly once per session" but doesn't define the scope of "session" (logged-out session only, or does it reset on re-login within the same browser).
onboarding.recommendations:
[1]. My recommendation: When a logged-out user's session expires (60 minutes of inactivity), the chat interface remains interactive but becomes unresponsive — attempts to send a message trigger an inline error message at the top of the chat stating "Your session has expired. Sign in to continue" with a "Sign in" call-to-action; no modal, no redirect, no forced state change; the user can dismiss the error and attempt to sign in at any time.

→ Rationale: Keeping the interface interactive respects user agency and prevents sudden disruption; an inline error is consistent with other error messaging in the spec; allowing the user to sign in on-demand rather than forcing a redirect preserves control.

→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

---

[2]. My recommendation: If a user's authentication token expires mid-session (server-side), the "Not signed in" indicator reappears automatically and all chat functionality becomes read-only (user can view conversation but cannot send new messages); attempting to send a message triggers an inline error stating "Your session has ended. Sign in again to continue"; the user can sign in again via the Auth Sheet without losing their conversation history.

→ Rationale: Reappearing the indicator makes the loss of auth state immediately visible; making chat read-only rather than fully unresponsive preserves conversation context; allowing re-sign-in without data loss respects user expectations and trust.

→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

---

[3]. My recommendation: The in-conversation nudge fires exactly once per logged-out session (defined as a single uninterrupted period of unauthenticated browsing); if the user signs in and then signs out again within the same browser window, that is a new session and the nudge fires again; the nudge is tied to the logged-out session, not to user lifetime or login count.

→ Rationale: Scoping "session" to uninterrupted logged-out periods aligns with AC#11 (fires exactly once) and prevents nudge fatigue; it respects that each exploration period is a distinct user intent; re-signing in/out creates a new intent context, so a new nudge is warranted.

→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

---

Say *yes* to apply these to the product spec and continue design, or reply to adjust any recommendation.
